6:["$","div",null,{"className":"max-w-7xl mx-auto px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"pt-6 serif"},"children":"How to Choose the Right Desk for Your Home Office"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/mia-collins","className":"flex items-center gap-2 object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"March 10, 2026"}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-6 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"Size, shape, material, storage — everything you need to weigh before buying the desk you will spend thousands of hours at."}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 mb-8 p-3 rounded-lg bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-900 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Affiliate disclosure:"}]," This post may contain affiliate links to products we recommend. We earn a small commission when you buy through them, at no extra cost to you. As an Amazon Associate we earn from qualifying purchases. See our ",["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/disclaimer","className":"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline","children":"disclaimer"}]," for details."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-most-important-piece-of-furniture-you-will-ever-buy","children":"The Most Important Piece of Furniture You Will Ever Buy"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your desk is the anchor of your entire workspace. Every other decision — chair height, monitor position, lighting angle — radiates outward from it. Yet most people spend more time choosing a sofa they sit on for two hours an evening than a desk they sit at for eight hours a day."}}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20","$L21","$L22","$L23"]}],"$L24","$L25","$L26"]}],"$L27"]}]]}]
7:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
8:["$","$L28",null,{"children":["$","$29",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@2a"}]}]
9:["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Getting the desk right is the single highest-leverage workspace decision you can make."}}]
a:["$","h2","3",{"id":"start-with-size","children":"Start with Size"}]
b:["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The minimum useful desk depth is 24 inches. Anything less and you cannot place a monitor at a proper viewing distance without it being right in your face. For a comfortable single-monitor setup, 27–30 inches of depth is ideal."}}]
c:["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Width depends on what you do. Writers and coders can get by with 48–54 inches. Designers, video editors, or anyone with a dual-monitor setup should look at 60–72 inches."}}]
d:["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Corner desks</strong> (L-shaped) offer the most usable surface area per square foot of floor space. The downside: they lock you into a layout and are harder to move. Only commit to a corner desk if you are certain about your room arrangement."}}]
e:["$","h2","7",{"id":"solid-vs-height-adjustable","children":"Solid vs. Height-Adjustable"}]
f:["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you spend more than six hours a day at your desk, a height-adjustable (sit-stand) desk is genuinely worth the investment. The ability to break up sitting with standing periods reduces fatigue measurably."}}]
10:["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Fixed-height desks are simpler, sturdier, and cheaper. If you are disciplined about getting up and moving regularly, a fixed desk is perfectly fine."}}]
11:["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The standard desk height is 29–30 inches</strong>, which is optimised for an average-height adult sitting in a standard chair. If you are notably taller or shorter than average, you will want either a height-adjustable desk or a desk with a non-standard height."}}]
12:["$","h2","11",{"id":"material-guide","children":"Material Guide"}]
13:["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Solid wood</strong>: Beautiful, durable, and heavy. Real hardwood desks (oak, walnut, maple) age well and last decades. Expensive but worth it if you treat the desk as a long-term investment."}}]
14:["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>MDF with laminate</strong>: The most common material in mid-range desks (IKEA's LINNMON is the classic example). Lightweight, affordable, and perfectly adequate. Not resistant to moisture or heavy impact."}}]
15:["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Bamboo</strong>: Increasingly popular in modern desks. Sustainable, hard, and attractive. A good middle ground between price and quality."}}]
16:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Metal</strong>: Steel-framed desks offer excellent durability and a clean industrial aesthetic. The top surface is usually wood, glass, or MDF."}}]
17:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Glass</strong>: Looks stunning in photos; is a fingerprint and scratch magnet in practice. Not recommended for daily use."}}]
18:["$","h2","17",{"id":"storage-decisions","children":"Storage Decisions"}]
19:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Built-in drawers reduce clutter but limit flexibility. A desk with no drawers combined with a separate filing cabinet or under-desk pedestal is often more versatile."}}]
1a:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you lean toward a clean, minimal setup, avoid built-in storage entirely. A clear desk surface is easier to maintain when there are no drawers tempting you to accumulate things."}}]
1b:["$","h2","20",{"id":"shape","children":"Shape"}]
1c:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Rectangular desks</strong> are the most flexible. They work in almost any room configuration and can be repositioned easily."}}]
1d:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>L-shaped desks</strong> maximise surface area and work well when you need to separate tasks spatially — primary work on one side, reference materials or a second screen on the other."}}]
1e:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Curved or wave-shaped desks</strong> look interesting but often result in wasted surface area and awkward ergonomic positions. Generally not worth the trade-off."}}]
1f:["$","h2","24",{"id":"what-to-look-for-in-a-frame","children":"What to Look For in a Frame"}]
20:["$","p","25",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The frame matters as much as the top. Look for:"}}]
21:["$","ul","26",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Thick legs or a crossbar</strong> for stability. A desk that wobbles when you type is exhausting."}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Cable management features</strong> — grommets, cable trays, or routing channels built in."}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Weight capacity</strong> — especially if you use a monitor arm or have heavy equipment."}}]]}]
22:["$","h2","27",{"id":"the-floor-test","children":"The Floor Test"}]
23:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Before buying, crouch down and look at the floor in your workspace. Is it level? Does it have rugs that will cause wobble? A desk that sits perfectly in a showroom may wobble on an uneven floor. Adjustable leg feet are a simple and often overlooked must-have."}}]
24:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Workspace","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-blue-50 text-blue-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Workspace"}],[["$","$L3","desk",{"href":"/blog/tag/desk","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","desk"]}],["$","$L3","buying-guide",{"href":"/blog/tag/buying-guide","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","buying-guide"]}],["$","$L3","home-office",{"href":"/blog/tag/home-office","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","home-office"]}],["$","$L3","setup-guide",{"href":"/blog/tag/setup-guide","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","setup-guide"]}]]]}]
25:["$","$L2b",null,{"title":"How to Choose the Right Desk for Your Home Office","slug":"how-to-choose-the-right-desk"}]
26:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L2c",null,{}]}]
27:["$","aside",null,{"className":"space-y-5 print:hidden","children":[["$","$L2d",null,{"items":[{"id":"the-most-important-piece-of-furniture-you-will-ever-buy","text":"The Most Important Piece of Furniture You Will Ever Buy","level":2},{"id":"start-with-size","text":"Start with Size","level":2},{"id":"solid-vs-height-adjustable","text":"Solid vs. Height-Adjustable","level":2},{"id":"material-guide","text":"Material Guide","level":2},{"id":"storage-decisions","text":"Storage Decisions","level":2},{"id":"shape","text":"Shape","level":2},{"id":"what-to-look-for-in-a-frame","text":"What to Look For in a Frame","level":2},{"id":"the-floor-test","text":"The Floor Test","level":2}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108377-be9c29b29330?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Mia Collins","className":"w-14 h-14 rounded-2xl object-cover object-top mb-4"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-lg text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 mb-0.5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Mia Collins"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-orange-500 font-medium mb-3","children":"Workspace Designer"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"A decade designing ergonomic workspaces for remote teams. 2e:["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/contact","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm font-semibold px-4 py-2.5 rounded-xl text-white bg-orange-500 hover:bg-orange-600 transition-colors","children":["Get in touch ",["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M7 7h10v10","key":"1tivn9"}],["path",{"d":"M7 17 17 7","key":"1vkiza"}]],"className":"lucide-arrow-up-right","size":14}]]}]
